title: Revert frontend logo branding to the originally uploaded asset

requirements.summary: Switch the app-wide logo source back to the originally uploaded logo via centralized branding config, ensuring Header/Footer/Home hero all render the same original asset.
acceptanceCriteria: The logo shown in the Header (BrandLogo variant="logo-with-text") matches the originally uploaded logo asset.
The logo shown in the Footer (BrandLogo variant="logo-with-text") matches the originally uploaded logo asset.
The logo shown on the Home page hero (BrandLogo variant="logo-with-text") matches the originally uploaded logo asset.
The app uses a single source of truth for the logo path (via frontend/src/config/branding.ts), with no remaining references that cause a different/incorrect logo to appear.

file_operations.description: Rename the originally uploaded logo asset to a stable, URL-safe filename for consistent usage across the app (e.g., rename to frontend/public/assets/escoffiers-logo-original.jpg).
Update BRANDING.logo to reference the originally uploaded logo asset (e.g., /assets/escoffiers-logo-original.jpg) as the single source of truth for all logo renderings.

Ensure all logo variants that render the logo image resolve their src exclusively from BRANDING.logo (no hardcoded/alternate logo paths), so Header/Footer/Home hero always display the centralized configured original asset.
Verify Header uses BrandLogo (variant="logo-with-text") without any alternate logo references so it reflects the updated BRANDING.logo everywhere.
Verify Footer uses BrandLogo (variant="logo-with-text") without any alternate logo references so it reflects the updated BRANDING.logo everywhere.
Verify the Home hero uses BrandLogo (variant="logo-with-text") without any alternate logo references so it reflects the updated BRANDING.logo everywhere.
